question: Explain the definition of thrust:

options.text: Thrust is a reaction force described quantitatively by Newton's third law, every force has an equal and opposite reaction.
Thrust is a reaction force described quantitatively by Newton's Second law, every force has an equal and opposite reaction.
Thrust is a reaction force described quantitatively by Newton's Second law, every force has a non-equal and non-opposite reaction.
Thrust is a reaction force described quantitatively by Newton's third law, every force has a non equal and non-opposite reaction.

classification_notes: Thrust is one of the four forces of flight.
